questions.question: State the two conditions required for two triangles to be similar.
questions.answer: Two triangles are similar if their corresponding angles are equal and their corresponding sides are proportional.

questions.question: Why are all equilateral triangles similar?
questions.answer: All equilateral triangles have all angles equal to 60° and their corresponding sides are proportional.

questions.question: State the Basic Proportionality Theorem.
questions.answer: If a line is drawn parallel to one side of a triangle to intersect the other two sides, it divides those sides in the same ratio.

questions.question: What is meant by the converse of the Basic Proportionality Theorem?
questions.answer: If a line divides two sides of a triangle in the same ratio, then it is parallel to the third side.

questions.question: Differentiate between congruent and similar triangles.
questions.answer: Congruent triangles have the same shape and size, whereas similar triangles have the same shape but not necessarily the same size.

questions.question: State the AAA similarity criterion.
questions.answer: If all corresponding angles of two triangles are equal, then the triangles are similar.

questions.question: State the AA similarity criterion.
questions.answer: If two angles of one triangle are equal to two angles of another triangle, then the triangles are similar.

questions.question: State the SSS similarity criterion.
questions.answer: If the corresponding sides of two triangles are proportional, then the triangles are similar.

questions.question: State the SAS similarity criterion.
questions.answer: If one angle of a triangle is equal to one angle of another triangle and the including sides are proportional, then the triangles are similar.

questions.question: What is meant by scale factor in similar triangles?
questions.answer: Scale factor is the ratio of the corresponding sides of two similar triangles.

questions.question: Why are corresponding angles equal in similar triangles?
questions.answer: Because similar triangles have the same shape, their corresponding angles are equal.

questions.question: Why are all circles similar?
questions.answer: All circles have the same shape irrespective of their radii.

questions.question: Can two right-angled triangles be similar? Give reason.
questions.answer: Yes, if their corresponding acute angles are equal, then they are similar.

questions.question: What happens to the ratio of areas of two similar triangles?
questions.answer: The ratio of their areas is equal to the square of the ratio of their corresponding sides.

questions.question: Why are a square and a rectangle not similar?
questions.answer: Though their angles are equal, their corresponding sides are not in the same ratio.

questions.question: Explain the importance of parallel lines in triangle similarity.
questions.answer: Parallel lines help in establishing proportional sides using the Basic Proportionality Theorem.

questions.question: What is meant by indirect measurement?
questions.answer: Indirect measurement is finding distances or heights using similarity instead of direct measurement.

questions.question: Why are corresponding sides proportional in similar triangles?
questions.answer: Because similar triangles are scaled versions of each other.

questions.question: What is meant by equiangular triangles?
questions.answer: Triangles whose corresponding angles are equal are called equiangular triangles.

questions.question: Why are similar figures not necessarily congruent?
questions.answer: Because similar figures may differ in size even though they have the same shape.

questions.question: What is meant by corresponding vertices?
questions.answer: Vertices that occupy the same relative position in similar figures are called corresponding vertices.

questions.question: Why is correct correspondence important in writing similarity statements?
questions.answer: Incorrect correspondence leads to wrong proportional relationships between sides.

questions.question: Can two isosceles triangles be always similar?
questions.answer: No, unless their corresponding angles are equal or sides are proportional.

questions.question: What is the role of medians in similar triangles?
questions.answer: Medians of similar triangles are proportional to the corresponding sides.

questions.question: Why is triangle similarity important in geometry?
questions.answer: It helps in solving problems related to height, distance, and scale.
